excerpts.evidence: SelectValue is the part that reflects the selected value. By default the selected item's text will be rendered. It should not be styled to ensure correct positioning.
excerpts.supportsGuideline: SelectValue must be inside SelectTrigger. It renders the current selection and placeholder. Do not style SelectValue — it will break positioning.

excerpts.evidence: SelectItem requires a value prop. SelectItemText should only contain the text you want to see in the trigger when that item is selected. It should not be styled to ensure correct positioning.
excerpts.supportsGuideline: SelectContent must contain SelectItem elements, each with a required value prop. Do not style SelectItemText — it breaks trigger display positioning.

excerpts.evidence: onValueChange is the event handler called when the value changes.
excerpts.supportsGuideline: Use onValueChange on the Select root to handle selection changes. Do not use onChange or onValueChanged — neither exists on this component.
